title: Xtra Smoky Mexican Chicken Tinga
description: A high-protein Mexican dish featuring smoky chicken, couscous, and spices, all wrapped in a pandan leaf, inspired by the flavors of Jalisco, with a Yakimono grill twist.
instructions: Preheat the Yakimono grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together chipotle peppers, olive oil, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the chicken to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Grill the chicken for 5-6 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the couscous according to package instructions.
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for a few minutes before shredding it into bite-sized pieces.
In a large skillet, heat a couple of tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat and saute the diced onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the shredded chicken to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the chicken mixture onto a pandan leaf, followed by a spoonful of couscous, and wrap the leaf to form a tight package.
Serve the Xtra Smoky Mexican Chicken Tinga hot, garnished with additional chipotle peppers if desired.
